sourceMovie.title: Ben-Hur
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Ben-Hur for the same reason it broke through in 1959: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. William Wyler keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The history and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Agora
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ben-Hur clicked for you, Agora (2009) is a strong next stop: both trade in history and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Risen
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ben-Hur clicked for you, Risen (2016) is a strong next stop: both trade in history and drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ben-Hur?
faq.answer: Try Agora, Risen, Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon—each shares DNA with Ben-Hur while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ben-Hur?
faq.answer: Ben-Hur (1959) is best described as History, Drama, Adventure. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ben-Hur worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.9/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this history lane.
